report_name: Krybit Ransomware Group Claims Attack on Thai Printing Company, Threatens Data Leak

threat_actors.name: Krybit
threat_actors.description: A ransomware group that claimed responsibility for a cyberattack on Smile Siam Printing Service, employing double-extortion tactics. The group operates a data leak site to pressure victims into paying ransoms.
threat_actors.goals: To extort money from victim organizations by encrypting their data and threatening to leak it.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: The Krybit group allegedly exfiltrated sensitive company data from Smile Siam Printing Service to their own servers before encryption to facilitate their double-extortion threat.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The Krybit ransomware encrypted files across the Smile Siam Printing Service network, rendering them inaccessible and disrupting business operations.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: The ransomware operators likely deleted volume shadow copies using tools like wmic.exe to hinder system recovery from local backups.

malware.name: Krybit Ransomware
malware.description: The ransomware payload used by the Krybit group to encrypt files and exfiltrate data from victims as part of a double-extortion scheme.

tools.name: wmic.exe
tools.description: Used to delete shadow copies to hinder system recovery, a common precursor activity for ransomware deployment.

identities.name: Smile Siam Printing Service
identities.description: A prominent printing company based in Thailand that was targeted by the Krybit ransomware group in a double-extortion attack.

indicators.name: WMIC Shadow Copy Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: WMIC Shadow Copy Deletion
id: 2b8a1c9d-5e6f-4a3b-8c1d-9e8f7a6b5c4d
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of wmic.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common technique used by ransomware to inhibit system recovery.
references:
  - https://www.dexpose.io/krybit-ransomware-strikes-smile-siam-printing-service/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-28
tags:
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.impact
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\wmic.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'shadowcopy'
      - 'delete'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative activity, though rare.
level: high
indicators.description: Detects the use of wmic.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common precursor to ransomware deployment used to hinder recovery.
